contentBlocks.original:
अथ श्रीरुद्रप्रश्नः ॥
श्री गुरुभ्यॊ नमः । हरिः ऒ३म् ।
ऒं गणानां त्वा गणपतिꣳ हवामहॆ कविं कवीनामुपमश्रवस्तमम् ।
ज्यॆष्ठराजं ब्रह्मणां ब्रह्मणस्पत आ नः श्ड़्ण्वन्नूतिभिस्सीद सादनम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha srīrudrapraśnaḥ
śrī gurubhyo namaḥ . hariḥ o3m .
oṃ gaṇānāṃ tvā gaṇapatigͫ havāmahe kaviṃ kavīnāmupamaśravastamam .
jyeṣṭharājaṃ brahmaṇāṃ brahmaṇaspata ā naḥ śṛṇvannūtibhissīda sādanam ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Now begins the Śrī Rudra Praśna.
Salutations to the revered Gurus. Oṃ, Hari, the supreme being.

Om, we invoke you, O Lord of the Ganas (attendants of Shiva).
The wisest among the wise, and the most famous of all.
The eldest Lord of all prayers, O Brahmanaspati (Lord of knowledge and prayers).
Hear our prayers and sit with us to grant your blessings.
contentBlocks.meanings:
atha śrīrudrapraśnaḥ: Now begins the Śrī Rudra Praśna.
atha: Now, or hereafter (indicating the beginning of something important).
śrīrudrapraśnaḥ: The Śrī Rudra Praśna (the recitation of the hymns dedicated to Rudra).
śrī gurubhyo namaḥ: Salutations to the revered Gurus.
śrī: A respectful prefix "revered" or "auspicious."
gurubhyaḥ: To the Gurus (teachers or spiritual guides).
namaḥ: Salutations or bowing down.
Next is the invocation that precedes the recitation of the Rudram. It is a prayer to Lord Ganesha, asking for his blessings to remove obstacles before the main ritual or prayer begins. Let's break it down and explain the line by line.
oṃ gaṇānāṃ tvā gaṇapatigͫ havāmahe: Om, we invoke you, O Lord of the Ganas (attendants of Shiva).
gaṇānāṃ: Of the Ganas (the divine attendants of Lord Shiva).
tvā: You (referring to Ganesha).
gaṇapatim: The Lord of the Ganas (Ganesha).
havāmahe: We invoke, or we call upon.
kaviṃ kavīnāmupamaśravastamam: The wisest among the wise, and the most famous of all.
kaviṃ: The wise one, or poet (Ganesha is known for his wisdom).
kavīnām: Among all the wise (Ganesha is the wisest among them).
upamaśravastamam: The most exalted or renowned for fame.
jyeṣṭharājaṃ brahmaṇāṃ brahmaṇaspata: The eldest Lord of all prayers, O Brahmanaspati (Lord of knowledge and prayers).
jyeṣṭharājaṃ: The eldest or foremost king.
brahmaṇāṃ: Of prayers or of knowledge (referring to Ganesha as the Lord of Vedic knowledge).
brahmaṇaspata: The Lord of prayers or knowledge, another name for Ganesha.
ā naḥ śṛṇvannūtibhissīda sādanam: Hear our prayers and sit with us to grant your blessings.
ā naḥ: To us.
śṛṇvan: Hear or listen to (our prayers).
ūtibhiḥ: Through the praises or hymns.
sīda: Be seated, or reside.
sādanam: At your seat, or in your abode (meaning, grant us your presence and blessings).

contentBlocks.original:
कृष्ण यजुर्वेदीय तैत्तिरीय संहिता
चतुर्थं-वैँश्वदेव-ङ्काण्ड-म्पञ्चमः प्रपाठकः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛṣṇa yajurvēdīya taittirīya saṃhitā
chaturthaṃ vaiśvadēva-ṅkāṇḍa-mpañchamaḥ prapāṭhakaḥ
contentBlocks.translation:
The Taittirīya Saṃhitā of the Kṛṣṇa Yajurveda.
The fifth section of the fourth Vaiśvadeva Kāṇḍa.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kṛṣṇa yajurvēdīya taittirīya saṃhitā: The Taittirīya Saṃhitā of the Kṛṣṇa Yajurveda.
kṛṣṇa yajurvēdīya: Of the Kṛṣṇa Yajurveda (one of the two branches of the Yajurveda).
taittirīya saṃhitā: The Taittirīya Saṃhitā (a Vedic text that is part of the Kṛṣṇa Yajurveda).
chaturthaṃ vaiśvadēva-ṅkāṇḍa-mpañchamaḥ prapāṭhakaḥ: The fifth section of the fourth Vaiśvadeva Kāṇḍa.
chaturtham: The fourth (referring to the fourth book or section).
vaiśvadēva-ṅkāṇḍa: The Vaiśvadeva Kāṇḍa (the section pertaining to the Vaiśvadevas, or all gods).
pañchamaḥ prapāṭhakaḥ: The fifth section or lesson.

contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ, salutations to the blessed one, Rudra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
.. oṃ namo bhagavate rudrāya ..: Oṃ, salutations to the blessed one, Rudra.
oṃ: The sacred sound, representing the essence of the universe.
namo: Salutations or bowing down.
bhagavate: To the blessed one, or the one endowed with divine qualities (referring to Rudra in this case).
rudrāya: To Rudra, the fierce and powerful form of Shiva.This verse is a salutation to Rudra, the fierce and destructive aspect of Shiva, who is also seen as a protector and healer. The invocation of Oṃ at the beginning sanctifies the prayer, and the term Bhagavate refers to the revered and blessed one, highlighting Rudra's divine qualities. This short yet powerful mantra is commonly used in the Vedic hymns to offer respect and invoke the grace of Rudra.

contentBlocks.original:
नम॑स्ते रुद्र म॒न्यव॑ उ॒तोत॒ इष॑वे॒ नमः॑ ।
नम॑स्ते अस्तु॒ धन्व॑ने बा॒हुभ्या॑मु॒त ते॒ नमः॑ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nama̍stē rudra ma̠nyava̍ u̠tōta̠ iṣa̍vē̠ nama̍ḥ ।
nama̍stē astu̠ dhanva̍nē bā̠hubhyā̍mu̠ta tē̠ nama̍ḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the wrath of Rudra and to his arrows.
Salutations to Rudra's bow and arms.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaste rudramanyava utota iṣave namaḥ: Salutations to your anger, O Rudra, and also to your arrow, salutations.
namaste: Salutations to you.
rudramanyava: O Rudra, (referring to your) anger or wrath.
utota: And also.
iṣave: To your arrow (a symbol of destruction and power).
namaḥ: Salutations or reverence.
namaste astu dhanvane bāhubhyāmuta te namaḥ: Salutations to your bow, and to your strong arms, salutations again to you.
namaste: Salutations to you.
astu: Let it be or may there be.
dhanvane: To your bow.
bāhubhyām: To your two arms.
uta: And also.
te namaḥ: Salutations to you.

contentBlocks.original:
या त॒ इषुः॑ शि॒वत॑मा शि॒व-म्ब॒भूव॑ ते॒ धनुः॑ ।
शि॒वा श॑र॒व्या॑ या तव॒ तया॑ नो रुद्र मृडय ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yā ta̠ iṣu̍-śśi̠vata̍mā śi̠va-mba̠bhūva̍ tē̠ dhanu̍ḥ ।
śi̠vā śa̍ra̠vyā̍ yā tava̠ tayā̍ nō rudra mṛḍaya ।
contentBlocks.translation: Let your arrow be most auspicious and your bow peaceful. With that most peaceful arrow of yours, O Rudra, make us happy.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yāta iṣuḥ śivatamā śivaṃ babhūva te dhanuḥ: May your arrow be most auspicious, and may your bow be a source of peace.
yāta: May it be.
iṣuḥ: The arrow.
śivatamā: Most auspicious or benevolent.
śivaṃ: Auspicious, peaceful.
babhūva: May it become.
te dhanuḥ: Your bow.
śivā śaravyā yā tava tayā no rudra mṛḍaya: May the quiver of arrows you carry also be auspicious; O Rudra, bless us with peace.
śivā: Auspicious or peaceful.
śaravyā: The quiver (holding arrows).
yā tava: That which is yours.
tayā: By that (instrument).
no: To us.
rudra: O Rudra (a fierce form of Shiva).
mṛḍaya: Make us happy, bless us, or give us peace.

contentBlocks.original:
या ते॑ रुद्र शि॒वा त॒नूरघो॒रा-ऽपा॑पकाशिनी ।
तया॑ नस्त॒नुवा॒ शन्त॑मया॒ गिरि॑शन्ता॒भिचा॑कशीहि ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yā tē̍ rudra śi̠vā ta̠nūraghō̠rā-'pā̍pakāśinī ।
tayā̍ nasta̠nuvā̠ śanta̍mayā̠ giri̍śantā̠bhichā̍kaśīhi ॥
contentBlocks.translation: That auspicious and benevolent form of yours, O Rudra, which destroys sins— with that peaceful form, O Giriśa, make yourself known to us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yā te rudra śivā tanūraghorā'pāpakāśinī: O Rudra, may your auspicious body, which destroys sins and wards off evil, be with us.
yā te: That which is yours.
rudra: O Rudra.
śivā: Auspicious or peaceful.
tanūḥ: Body or form.
aghora: Non-fierce, gentle, or non-terrifying.
apāpakāśinī: The one who destroys sins or evil.
tayā nastanuvā śantamayā giriśaṃtābhicākaśīhi: By that peaceful form of yours, O Lord of the mountains, make your presence known to us.
tayā: By that.
naḥ: To us.
tanuvā: With your body or form.
śantamayā: Full of peace, calm, or tranquility.
giriśa: Lord of the mountains (a title for Lord Shiva, the resident of Mount Kailash).
abhicākaśīhi: Reveal yourself, make your presence known.

contentBlocks.original:
यामिषु॑-ङ्गिरिशन्त॒ हस्ते॒ बिभ॒र्​ष्यस्त॑वे ।
शि॒वा-ङ्गि॑रित्र॒ ता-ङ्कु॑रु॒ मा हिग्ं॑सीः॒ पुरु॑ष॒-ञ्जग॑त्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāmiṣu̍-ṅgiriśanta̠ hastē̠ bibha̠r​ṣyasta̍vē ।
śi̠vā-ṅgi̍ritra̠ tā-ṅku̍ru̠ mā hig̍ṃsī̠ḥ puru̍ṣa̠-ñjaga̍t॥
contentBlocks.translation: That arrow you hold in your hand, O Giriśa, make it peaceful. Do not harm the world or its people.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yāmiṣuṃ giriśaṃta haste bibharṣyastave: O Lord of the mountains, the arrow you carry in your hand.
yāmiṣuṃ: The arrow.
giriśa: Lord of the mountains (Shiva).
haste: In your hand.
bibharṣi: You hold or carry.
astave: For shooting or releasing.
śivāṃ giritra tāṃ kuru mā higͫsīḥ puruṣaṃ jagat: O protector of the mountains, make that arrow peaceful, and do not harm either man or the world.
śivāṃ: Peaceful or auspicious.
giritra: Protector of the mountains (another title of Lord Shiva).
tāṃ kuru: Make it (the arrow).
mā: Do not.
higͫsīḥ: Harm or injure.
puruṣaṃ: Man or person.
jagat: The world or creation.

contentBlocks.original:
शि॒वेन॒ वच॑सा त्वा॒ गिरि॒शाच्छा॑ वदामसि ।
यथा॑ न॒-स्सर्व॒मिज्जग॑दय॒क्ष्मग्ं सु॒मना॒ अस॑त् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śi̠vēna̠ vacha̍sā tvā̠ giri̠śāchChā̍ vadāmasi ।
yathā̍ na̠-ssarva̠mijjaga̍daya̠kṣmagṃ su̠manā̠ asa̍t ॥
contentBlocks.translation: We invoke you, O Giriśa, with auspicious words so that the whole world may be free from disease and suffering.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śivena vacasā tvā giriśācchā vadāmasi: By auspicious words, we speak to you, O Lord of the mountains (Giriśa).
śivena vacasā: By auspicious or peaceful words.
tvā: To you.
giriśa: O Lord of the mountains (a name for Shiva).
acchā vadāmasi: We speak or address.
yathā naḥ sarvamijjagadayakṣmasumanā asat: So that the entire world, free from disease, may be full of good thoughts.
yathā: So that.
naḥ: For us.
sarvam: The entire.
idam jagat: This world.
ayakṣma: Free from disease or affliction.
sumanāḥ asat: May it become full of good thoughts or peaceful minds.

contentBlocks.original:
अध्य॑वोचदधिव॒क्ता प्र॑थ॒मो दैव्यो॑ भि॒षक् ।
अहीग्॑श्च॒ सर्वा᳚ञ्ज॒म्भय॒न्-थ्सर्वा᳚श्च यातुधा॒न्यः॑ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
adhya̍vocadadhiva̱ktā pra̍tha̱mo daivyo̍ bhi̱ṣak .
ahīg̍śca̱ sarvā̎ñja̱mbhaya̱n-thsarvā̎śca yātudhā̱nya̍ḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation: The first divine healer said, "May Rudra remove all the serpents and other harmful beings."
contentBlocks.meanings:
adhyavocadadhi vaktā prathamo daivyo bhiṣak: The divine healer, the first physician of the gods, has spoken.
adhyavocat: Has spoken or declared.
adhi vaktā: The one who speaks or declares.
prathamaḥ: The first.
daivyaḥ bhiṣak: The divine healer or physician.
ahīśca sarvāñjambhayantsarvāśca yātudhānyaḥ: He who destroys all snakes and all evil beings (yātudhānas).
ahīś: Snakes or serpents.
ca sarvān: And all.
jambhayat: Destroys or subdues.
sarvāśca: And all.
yātudhānyaḥ: Evil spirits or beings.

contentBlocks.original:
अ॒सौ यस्ता॒म्रो अ॑रु॒ण उ॒त ब॒भ्रुस्सु॑म॒ङ्गलः॑ ।
ये चे॒माग्ं रु॒द्रा अ॒भितो॑ दि॒क्षु श्रि॒ता-स्स॑हस्र॒शो-ऽवैषा॒ग्ं॒ हेड॑ ईमहे ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
a̠sau yastā̠mrō a̍ru̠ṇa u̠ta ba̠bhrussu̍ma̠ṅgala̍ḥ ।
yē chē̠māgṃ ru̠drā a̠bhitō̍ di̠kṣu śri̠tā-ssa̍hasra̠śō-'vaiṣā̠gṃ̠ hēḍa̍ īmahē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The one who is copper-red, reddish-brown, and auburn-hued, very auspicious, and the Rudras that are spread in all directions, may they remove the harm from us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
asau yastāmro aruṇa uta babhruḥ sumaṃgalaḥ: That (form of Rudra) which is copper-colored, reddish, and brown, and is most auspicious.
asau yaḥ: That one who.
tāmraḥ: Is copper-colored.
aruṇaḥ: Is reddish.
babhruḥ: Is brown.
sumaṃgalaḥ: Is most auspicious.
ye cemārudrā abhito dikṣu śritāḥ sahasraśo'vaiṣāheḍa īmahe: Thousands of Rudras, who have taken positions in all directions, may they not harm us.
ye: Those who.
ca imā: These.
rudrāḥ: Rudras (manifestations of Shiva).
abhitaḥ: Around or in all directions.
dikṣu: Directions.
śritāḥ: Are positioned or stationed.
sahasraśaḥ: In thousands.
avaiṣāḥ: May they protect.
heḍaḥ: Harm or disturbance.
īmahe: We seek to avoid or protect ourselves from.

contentBlocks.original:
अ॒सौ यो॑-ऽव॒सर्प॑ति॒ नील॑ग्रीवो॒ विलो॑हितः ।
उ॒तैन॑-ङ्गो॒पा अ॑दृश॒न्नदृ॑शन्नुदहा॒र्यः॑ ।
उ॒तैनं॒-विँश्वा॑ भू॒तानि॒ स दृ॒ष्टो मृ॑डयाति नः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
a̠sau yō̍-'va̠sarpa̍ti̠ nīla̍grīvō̠ vilō̍hitaḥ ।
u̠taina̍-ṅgō̠pā a̍dṛśa̠nnadṛ̍śannudahā̠rya̍ḥ ।
u̠taina̠ṃ viśvā̍ bhū̠tāni̠ sa dṛ̠ṣṭō mṛ̍ḍayāti naḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The blue-throated, red-hued one moves through the world. He is seen by the cowherds and all beings. When seen, he brings protection to us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
asau yo'vasarpati nīlagrīvo vilohitaḥ: That one (Rudra) who descends with a blue neck and a red body.
asau yaḥ: That one who.
avasarpati: Descends or moves down.
nīlagrīvaḥ: With a blue neck (referring to Shiva as Neelakantha).
vilohitaḥ: With a red body.
utainaṃ gopā adṛśannadṛśannudahāryaḥ: The cowherds saw him, and so did the women carrying water.
uta enaṃ: And him.
gopāḥ: The cowherds.
adṛśan: Saw.
adṛśan: Saw again (repetition for emphasis).
udahāryaḥ: Water-carrying women or those who draw water.
utainaṃ viśvā bhūtāni sa dṛṣṭo mṛḍayāti naḥ: All beings have seen him, and he, having been seen, grants us peace.
uta enaṃ: And him.
viśvā bhūtāni: All beings.
saḥ dṛṣṭaḥ: Having been seen.
mṛḍayāti: Grants us peace or blesses us.
naḥ: To us.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो॑ अस्तु॒ नील॑ग्रीवाय सहस्रा॒क्षाय॑ मी॒ढुषे᳚ ।
अथो॒ ये अ॑स्य॒ सत्वा॑नो॒-ऽह-न्तेभ्यो॑-ऽकर॒न्नमः॑ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō̍ astu̠ nīla̍grīvāya sahasrā̠kṣāya̍ mī̠ḍhuṣē̎ ।
athō̠ yē a̍sya̠ satvā̍nō̠-'ha-ntēbhyō̍-'kara̠nnama̍ḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the blue-throated one, the thousand-eyed one who showers blessings. Salutations to those who follow him.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo astu nīlagrīvāya sahasrākṣāya mīḍhuṣe: Salutations to the blue-necked one, the thousand-eyed one, the bestower of blessings.
namaḥ astu: Salutations to.
nīlagrīvāya: The blue-necked one (Shiva).
sahasrākṣāya: The thousand-eyed one (indicating omnipresence).
mīḍhuṣe: The bestower of blessings.
atho ye asya satvāno'haṃ tebhyo'karannamaḥ: And to his attendants (satvāna), I offer my salutations.
atho: And.
ye asya: Those who are his.
satvānaḥ: Attendants or beings associated with him.
aham: I.
tebhyaḥ: To them.
akaram namaḥ: I offer salutations.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रमुंच धन्वनस्त्वमुभयॊरार्त्नियॊर्ज्याम् ।
याश्च तॆ हस्त इषवः परा ता भगवॊ वप ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pramu̍ñcha̠ dhanva̍na̠stvamu̠bhayō̠rārtni̍ yō̠rjyām ।
yāścha̍ tē̠ hasta̠ iṣa̍va̠ḥ parā̠ tā bha̍gavō vapa ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Release the string of your bow from both ends, O Lord, and may the arrows in your hand be far away from us, O Lord.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pramuṃca dhanvanastvamubhayorārtniyorjyām: Release the bowstring from both ends of your bow.
pramuṃca: Release or loosen.
dhanvanaḥ: Of the bow.
tvam: You.
ubhayoh: Of both.
ārtniyoḥ: Ends or limbs (of the bow).
jyām: The bowstring.
yāśca te hasta iṣavaḥ parā tā bhagavo vapa: And the arrows in your hand, O Lord, please cast them away.
yāḥ ca: And those which.
te hasta: Are in your hand.
iṣavaḥ: The arrows.
parā tā: Cast them away.
bhagavaḥ: O Lord.
vapa: Throw or cast away.

contentBlocks.original:
अ॒व॒तत्य॒ धनु॒स्त्वग्ं सह॑स्राक्ष॒ शते॑षुधे ।
नि॒शीर्य॑ श॒ल्याना॒-म्मुखा॑ शि॒वो नः॑ सु॒मना॑ भव ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
a̠va̠tatya̠ dhanu̠stvagṃ saha̍srākṣa̠ śatē̍ṣudhē ।
ni̠śīrya̍ śa̠lyānā̠-mmukhā̍ śi̠vō na̍-ssu̠manā̍ bhava ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O thousand-eyed Lord, lower your bow and keep its arrows away from us. Be peaceful and kind to us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
avatatya dhanustva sahasrākṣa śateṣudhe: Lower your bow, O thousand-eyed one, with a hundred arrows.
avatatya: Lower or unstring.
dhanuḥ: The bow.
tvam: You.
sahasrākṣa: O thousand-eyed one (Shiva).
śateṣu-dhe: With hundreds of arrows.
niśīrya śalyānāṃ mukhā śivo naḥ sumanā bhava: Break the tips of your arrows, and be peaceful and kind to us.
niśīrya: Break or blunt.
śalyānām: Of the arrow tips.
mukhāḥ: The tips or heads.
śivaḥ: Auspicious or peaceful.
naḥ: To us.
sumanāḥ bhava: Be kind and well-disposed.

contentBlocks.original:
विज्य॒-न्धनुः॑ कप॒र्दिनो॒ विश॑ल्यो॒ बाण॑वाग्ं उ॒त ।
अने॑शन्न॒स्येष॑व आ॒भुर॑स्य निष॒ङ्गथिः॑ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vijya̠-ndhanu̍ḥ kapa̠rdinō̠ viśa̍lyō̠ bāṇa̍vāgṃ u̠ta ।
anē̍śanna̠syēṣa̍va ā̠bhura̍sya niṣa̠ṅgathi̍ḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The unstrung bow of Kapardin (one with matted hair), with arrows removed, is kept aside. The quiver is also set aside for the protection of the world.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vijyaṃ dhanuḥ kapardino viśalyo bāṇavā uta: O Kapardin (Shiva with matted hair), keep your bow unstrung and without arrows.
vijyam: Unstrung.
dhanuḥ: Bow.
kapardino: O Kapardin (Shiva with matted hair).
viśalyaḥ: Without arrowheads.
bāṇavān: With arrows.
uta: And also.
aneśannasyeṣava ābhurasya niṣaṃgathiḥ: Let your quiver be free of arrows, and let your bow rest peacefully.
aneśat: Let it be free.
asya iṣavaḥ: His arrows.
ābhuraḥ: Let the bow rest.
asya niṣaṃgathiḥ: The quiver of arrows.

contentBlocks.original:
या ते॑ हे॒तिर्मी॑डुष्टम॒ हस्ते॑ ब॒भूव॑ ते॒ धनुः॑ ।
तया॒-ऽस्मान्, वि॒श्वत॒स्त्वम॑य॒क्ष्मया॒ परि॑ब्भुज ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yā tē̍ hē̠tirmī̍ḍuṣṭama̠ hastē̍ ba̠bhūva̍ tē̠ dhanu̍ḥ ।
tayā̠-'smān, vi̠śvata̠stvama̍ya̠kṣmayā̠ pari̍bbhuja ॥
contentBlocks.translation: That weapon in your hand, O most gracious one, that bow—protect us from all directions and make us free from disease.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yā te hetirmīḍhuṣṭama haste babhūva te dhanuḥ: That weapon of yours in your hand, O bestower of blessings, may your bow protect us from all directions.
yā te hetiḥ: That weapon of yours.
mīḍhuṣṭama: O bestower of blessings.
haste: In your hand.
babhūva: Has been.
te dhanuḥ: Your bow.
tayā'smānviśvatastvamayakṣmayā paribbhuja: By that bow, protect us from all directions with peace and without afflictions.
tayā: By that (bow).
asmān: Us.
viśvataḥ: From all directions.
tvam: You.
ayakṣmayā: Without afflictions or disease.
paribbhuja: Protect or envelop.

contentBlocks.original:
नम॑स्ते अ॒स्त्वायु॑धा॒याना॑तताय धृ॒ष्णवे᳚ ।
उ॒भाभ्या॑मु॒त ते॒ नमो॑ बा॒हुभ्या॒-न्तव॒ धन्व॑ने ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nama̍stē a̠stvāyu̍dhā̠yānā̍tatāya dhṛ̠ṣṇavē̎ ।
u̠bhābhyā̍mu̠ta tē̠ namō̍ bā̠hubhyā̠-ntava̠ dhanva̍nē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to your mighty weapon, O Rudra. Salutations to both your arms and your bow, which are powerful and victorious.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaste astvāyudhāyānātatāya dhṛṣṇave: Salutations to your weapon, whether strung or unstrung, O fierce one.
namaḥ te: Salutations to you.
āyudhāya: To your weapon.
anātatāya: Whether strung or unstrung.
dhṛṣṇave: O fierce one.
ubhābhyāmuta te namo bāhubhyāṃ tava dhanvane: Salutations to your two arms and your bow.
ubhābhyām: To both (your arms).
uta: And also.
te namaḥ: Salutations to you.
bāhubhyām: To your two arms.
tava dhanvane: And your bow.

contentBlocks.original:
परि॑ ते॒ धन्व॑नो हे॒तिर॒स्मान् वृ॑णक्तु वि॒श्वतः॑ ।
अथो॒ य इ॑षु॒धिस्तवा॒रे अ॒स्मन्निधे॑हि॒ तम् ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pari̍ tē̠ dhanva̍nō hē̠tira̠smān vṛ̍ṇaktu vi̠śvata̍ḥ ।
athō̠ ya i̍ṣu̠dhistavā̠rē a̠smannidhē̍hi̠ tam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the arrows of your bow not harm us from all sides. And may your quiver be placed far away from us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pari te dhanvano hetirasmānvruṇaktu viśvataḥ: May the arrows of your bow spare us from all directions.
pari: Around or surrounding.
te dhanvanaḥ: Your bow.
hetiḥ: The arrows or missiles.
asmān: Us.
vruṇaktu: Spare or protect.
viśvataḥ: From all directions.
atho ya iṣudhistavāre asmannidhehi tam: And place your quiver of arrows far away from us.
atho: And.
yaḥ iṣudhiḥ: That quiver of arrows.
tava: Yours.
āre: Far away.
asmān: From us.
nidhehi tam: Place or keep it.

contentBlocks.original:
श्री शम्भ॑वे॒ नमः॑ ॥
नम॑स्ते अस्तु भगवन्-विश्वेश्व॒राय॑ महादे॒वाय॑ त्र्यम्ब॒काय॑ त्रिपुरान्त॒काय॑
त्रिकाग्निका॒लाय॑ कालाग्निरु॒द्राय॑ नीलक॒ण्ठाय॑
मृत्युञ्ज॒याय॑ सर्वेश्व॒राय॑ सदाशि॒वाय॑ [शङ्क॒राय॑] श्रीम-न्महादे॒वाय॒ नमः॑ ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī śambha̍vē̠ nama̍ḥ ॥
nama̍stē astu bhagavan-viśvēśva̠rāya̍ mahādē̠vāya̍ tryamba̠kāya̍ tripurānta̠kāya̍ trikāgnikā̠lāya̍ kālāgniru̠drāya̍ nīlaka̠ṇṭhāya̍ mṛtyuñja̠yāya̍ sarvēśva̠rāya̍ sadāśi̠vāya̍ [śaṅka̠rāya̍] śrīma-nmahādē̠vāya̠ nama̍ḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: This verse is a powerful invocation to Lord Shiva, calling him by many of his divine names and forms. It begins by addressing him as Viśveśvara (the lord of the universe) and Mahadeva (the great god), and continues to praise his other aspects, including Tryambaka (the three-eyed one), Tripurāntaka (the destroyer of the demon Tripura), and Kālāgnirudra (the fire that consumes time). Shiva is also praised as Nīlakaṇṭha (the blue-throated one, who saved the world by swallowing poison), and as Mṛtyuṃjaya (the conqueror of death). Finally, the verse addresses him as Sarveśvara (the lord of all beings) and Sadāśiva (the ever-auspicious one), concluding with an offering of salutations to Śrīman Mahādeva (the glorious great god).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaste astu bhagavan viśveśvarāya mahādevāya tryambakāya: Salutations to the blessed Lord, the ruler of the universe, the great god, and the three-eyed one.
namaste astu: Salutations to you.
bhagavan: O blessed Lord (a term of reverence for Shiva).
viśveśvarāya: To the Lord of the universe.
mahādevāya: To the great god (Mahadeva, a title of Shiva).
tryambakāya: To the three-eyed one (a name for Shiva, referring to his third eye of wisdom).
tripurāntakāya trikāgnikālāya kālāgnirudrāya: To the destroyer of Tripura, the one who controls the three fires of time, and the Rudra who is the fire of destruction.
tripurāntakāya: To the destroyer of Tripura (the three cities, referring to the demon cities destroyed by Shiva).
trikāgnikālāya: To the one who controls the three fires of time (past, present, and future).
kālāgnirudrāya: To Rudra, the fire of time (who destroys at the end of time).
trikālāgni nīlakaṇṭhāya mṛtyuṃjayāya: To the one who has a blue throat, who conquers death, and who controls the three times (past, present, and future).
trikālāgni: To the one who controls the three fires or the three times (past, present, and future).
nīlakaṇṭhāya: To the one with a blue throat (Shiva, referring to his holding of the poison during the churning of the ocean).
mṛtyuṃjayāya: To the conqueror of death (another name for Shiva, the one who grants victory over death).
sarveśvarāya sadāśivāya śrīmanmahādevāya namaḥ: To the lord of all, to the eternal Shiva, to the glorious great god, salutations.
sarveśvarāya: To the lord of all (referring to Shiva’s supreme lordship over everything).
sadāśivāya: To the ever-auspicious one (another name for Shiva, representing his eternal and benevolent form).
śrīmanmahādevāya: To the glorious great god (Mahadeva, a reverent title for Shiva).
namaḥ: Salutations.

contentBlocks.original:
2nd Anuvaka
नमो॒ हिर॑ण्य बाहवे सेना॒न्ये॑ दि॒शा-ञ्च॒ पत॑ये॒ नमो॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
2nd Anuvaka
namō̠ hira̍ṇya bāhavē sēnā̠nyē̍ di̠śā-ñcha̠ pata̍yē̠ namō̠
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the golden-armed one, the commander of armies, and the lord of all directions.
contentBlocks.meanings:
hiraṇyabāhave: To the golden-armed one (referring to Lord Rudra with arms shining like gold).
senānye: Commander of armies.
diśāṃ ca pataye: Lord of all directions.
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the trees with green leaves and to the lord of all animals.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vṛkṣebhyaḥ: To the trees.
harikeśebhyaḥ: To the ones with green leaves (referring to Rudra as the essence of life in nature).
paśūnāṃ pataye: To the lord of animals (Shiva is also the protector of all living creatures).
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one with tawny-colored hair, who shines brightly, and to the lord of the paths.
contentBlocks.meanings:
saspiñcarāya: To the one with tawny or golden hair.
tviṣīmate: To the radiant one (referring to Rudra's brightness or brilliance).
pathīnāṃ pataye: To the lord of the paths (Rudra is the protector of those on the journey).
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the brown-hued one, who pierces with arrows, and to the lord of food.
contentBlocks.meanings:
babhluśāya: To the brown-hued one (Rudra's form in nature).
vivyādhine: The one who pierces (with arrows or his gaze).
annānāṃ pataye: To the lord of food (Shiva is the provider of nourishment).
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one with green hair, wearing the sacred thread, and to the lord of those who are well-nourished.
contentBlocks.meanings:
harikeśāya: To the one with green hair (symbolizing nature or fertility).
upavītine: To the one wearing the sacred thread (referring to the purity of Rudra).
puṣṭānāṃ pataye: To the lord of the well-nourished (those who are healthy and prosperous).
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the weapons of Bhava (Shiva) and to the lord of the universe.
contentBlocks.meanings:
bhavasya hetyai: To the weapons of Bhava (another name for Shiva, referring to his tools of destruction).
jagatāṃ pataye: To the lord of the universe.
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Rudra, the wielder of the drawn bow, and to the lord of the fields.
contentBlocks.meanings:
rudrāya: To Rudra (Shiva in his fierce form).
ātatāvine: The one who holds a drawn bow.
kṣetrāṇāṃ pataye: To the lord of the fields (Shiva as the protector of agricultural lands).
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the charioteer, to the one who destroys, and to the lord of forests.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sūtāya: To the charioteer.
ahantyāya: To the one who destroys.
vanānāṃ pataye: To the lord of forests (Shiva as the protector of forests and wild areas).
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the red-hued one, to the lord of carpenters, and to the lord of trees.
contentBlocks.meanings:
rohitāya: To the red-hued one (another aspect of Rudra, possibly in his fiery form).
sthapataye: To the lord of carpenters or builders.
vṛkṣāṇāṃ pataye: To the lord of trees.
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the counselor, to the merchant, and to the lord of enclosures.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mantriṇe: To the counselor (Shiva as the giver of wisdom and counsel).
vāṇijāya: To the merchant (Shiva as the protector of commerce and trade).
kakṣāṇāṃ pataye: To the lord of enclosures (possibly gardens, fortresses, or settlements).
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who bestows growth, who brings forth water, and to the lord of medicinal herbs.
contentBlocks.meanings:
bhuvaṃtaye: To the one who bestows growth or increases the earth's fertility.
vārivaskṛtāya: To the one who brings forth water (as in rain or sustenance).
auṣadhīnāṃ pataye: To the lord of medicinal herbs (Shiva as the protector of healing plants).
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who makes loud sounds, who cries out mightily, and to the lord of foot soldiers.
contentBlocks.meanings:
uccairghoṣāya: To the one who makes loud sounds or calls.
ākrandayate: To the one who cries out or calls out loudly (possibly referring to the war cry or invocation).
pattīnāṃ pataye: To the lord of foot soldiers.
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the all-pervading one who runs swiftly and to the lord of living beings.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kṛtsnavītāya: To the all-pervading one.
dhāvate: To the one who runs swiftly.
satvanāṃ pataye: To the lord of living beings or warriors.
namaḥ: Salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the tolerant one who inflicts wounds, and to the lord of those who cause diseases.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sahamānāya: To the tolerant one (Rudra's aspect of enduring or bearing).
nivyādhina: To the one who inflicts wounds.
āvyādhinīnāṃ pataye: To the lord of those who cause diseases.
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one with prominent shoulders who bears a sword, and to the lord of thieves.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kakubhāya: To the one with prominent shoulders (referring to Rudra's strength).
niṣaṅgiṇe: To the one bearing a sword.
stenānāṃ pataye: To the lord of thieves.
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one bearing a sword and a quiver of arrows, and to the lord of robbers.
contentBlocks.meanings:
niṣaṅgiṇa: To the one bearing a sword.
iṣudhimate: To the one with a quiver (of arrows).
taskarāṇāṃ pataye: To the lord of robbers.
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the deceiver and the great deceiver, and to the lord of those who steal.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vañcate: To the deceiver.
parivañcate: To the great deceiver.
stāyūnāṃ pataye: To the lord of those who steal.
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who moves about in hiding and who prowls around, and to the lord of those who live in the forests.
contentBlocks.meanings:
nicerave: To the one who moves in hiding.
paricarāya: To the one who moves around or prowls.
āraṇyānāṃ pataye: To the lord of the forests.
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who use traps and snares and to those who are eager to seize, and to the lord of those who steal.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sṛkāvibhyo: To those who use traps or snares.
jighāsadbhyo: To those who are eager to seize.
muṣṇatāṃ pataye: To the lord of those who steal.
namo namo: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who carry swords and to those who roam at night, and to the lord of those who cut down and plunder.
contentBlocks.meanings:
'simadbhyo: To those who carry swords.
naktaṃ caradbhyaḥ: To those who roam at night.
prakṛntānāṃ pataye: To the lord of those who cut down or plunder.
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who wears a turban and who moves about in the mountains, and to the lord of birds.
contentBlocks.meanings:
uṣṇīṣiṇe: To the one who wears a turban (a reference to Rudra in disguise or protection).
giricarāya: To the one who moves about in the mountains.
kuluñcānāṃ pataye: To the lord of birds (especially those that live in the mountains).
namo namaḥ: Repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who carry arrows and to those who wield bows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
iṣumadbhyaḥ: To those who carry arrows.
dhanvāvibhyaḥ: To those who wield bows.
ca vo namo nama: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who stretch their bows and to those who set them down.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ātanvānebhyaḥ: To those who stretch their bows.
pratidadhānebhyaḥ: To those who set their bows down.
ca vo namo nama: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who draw their bows and to those who release arrows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āyacchadbhyaḥ: To those who draw their bows.
visṛjadbhyaḥ: To those who release arrows.
ca vo namo namo: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who shoot and to those who pierce.
contentBlocks.meanings:
'syadbhyaḥ: To those who shoot.
viddhyadbhyaḥ: To those who pierce.
ca vo namo nama: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who are seated and to those who lie down.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āsīnebhyaḥ: To those who are seated.
śayānebhyaḥ: To those who lie down.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who sleep and to those who are awake.
contentBlocks.meanings:
svapadbhyaḥ: To those who sleep.
jāgradbhyaḥ: To those who are awake.
ca vo namo nama: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who stand still and to those who run.
contentBlocks.meanings:
stiṣṭhadbhyaḥ: To those who stand still.
dhāvadbhyaḥ: To those who run.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who are in assemblies and to the lords of those assemblies.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sabhābhyaḥ: To those who are in assemblies.
sabhāpatibhyaḥ: To the lords of those assemblies.
ca vo namo: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the horses and to the lords of horses.
contentBlocks.meanings:
aśvebhyaḥ: To the horses.
aśvapatibhyaḥ: To the lords of horses.
ca vo namaḥ: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.original:
4th Anuvaka
नम॑ आव्या॒धिनी᳚भ्यो वि॒विध्य॑न्तीभ्यश्च वो॒ नमो॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
4th Anuvaka
nama̍ āvyā̠dhinī̎bhyō vi̠vidhya̍ntībhyaścha vō̠ namō̠
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who cause disease and to those who pierce with arrows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āvyādhinībhyaḥ: To those who cause diseases.
vividhyantībhyaḥ: To those who pierce (with arrows or weapons).
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the powerful ones and to the strong and overwhelming ones.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ugaṇābhyaḥ: To the powerful ones.
tṛhatībhyaḥ: To those who are strong and overwhelming.
ca vo namo namo: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who seek wealth and to the lords of those who seek wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings:
gṛtsebhyaḥ: To those who seek wealth.
grutsapatibhyaḥ: To the lords of those who seek wealth.
ca vo namo namo: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the troops and to the lords of troops.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vrātebhyaḥ: To the troops.
vrātapatibhyaḥ: To the lords of troops.
ca vo namo namo: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the groups and to the lords of groups.
contentBlocks.meanings:
gaṇebhyaḥ: To the groups (or crowds).
gaṇapatibhyaḥ: To the lords of groups (leaders).
ca vo namo namo: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those of deformed shapes and to those of diverse forms.
contentBlocks.meanings:
virūpebhyaḥ: To those of deformed or strange shapes.
viśvarūpebhyaḥ: To those of diverse forms (those who assume all forms).
ca vo namo namo: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the great ones and to the small ones.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mahadbhyaḥ: To the great ones.
kṣullakebhyaḥ: To the small ones.
ca vo namo namo: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the charioteers and to those without chariots.
contentBlocks.meanings:
rathibhyaḥ: To the charioteers.
arathebhyaḥ: To those without chariots.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the chariots and to the lords of chariots.
contentBlocks.meanings:
rathebhyaḥ: To the chariots.
rathapatibhyaḥ: To the lords of chariots.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the armies and to the leaders of armies.
contentBlocks.meanings:
senābhyaḥ: To the armies.
senanibhyaḥ: To the leaders of armies.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who protect and to those who gather.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kṣattṛbhyaḥ: To those who protect (warriors or bodyguards).
saṃgrahītṛbhyaḥ: To those who gather (those who collect or assemble).
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the carpenters and to the makers of chariots.
contentBlocks.meanings:
stakṣabhyaḥ: To the carpenters.
rathakārebhyaḥ: To the makers of chariots.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the potters and to the blacksmiths.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kulālebhyaḥ: To the potters.
karmārebhyaḥ: To the blacksmiths.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the fowlers and to the Nishadas.
contentBlocks.meanings:
puñjiṣṭebhyaḥ: To the fowlers (those who catch birds).
niṣādebhyaḥ: To the Nishadas (a class of hunters or forest dwellers).
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the makers of arrows and to the makers of bows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
iṣukṛdbhyaḥ: To the makers of arrows.
dhanvakṛdbhyaḥ: To the makers of bows.
ca vo namo namo: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the hunters and to the keepers of dogs.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mrugayubhyaḥ: To the hunters.
śvanibhyaḥ: To the keepers of dogs.
ca vo namo namaḥ: And to you, repeated salutations.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the dogs and to the lords of dogs.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śvabhyaḥ: To the dogs.
śvapatibhyaḥ: To the lords of dogs.
ca vo namaḥ: And to you, salutations.

contentBlocks.original:
5th Anuvaka
नमो॑ भ॒वाय॑ च रु॒द्राय॑ च॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
5th Anuvaka
namō̍ bha̠vāya̍ cha ru̠drāya̍ cha̠
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Bhava, the source of existence, and to Rudra, the fierce one.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
bhavāya: To Bhava, the source of existence (another name for Shiva, representing creation and growth).
rudrāya: To Rudra, the fierce one (Shiva in his destructive form).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sharva, the destroyer, and to Paśupati, the lord of all living beings.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
śarvāya: To Sharva, the destroyer (a fierce form of Shiva).
paśupataye: To Paśupati, the lord of all living beings.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one with a blue throat and to the one with a white neck.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
nīlagrīvāya: To the one with a blue throat (Shiva, who drank the poison during the churning of the ocean).
śitikaṇṭhāya: To the one with a white neck.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the matted-haired one and to the one with shaven hair.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
kapardine: To the one with matted hair (Shiva in his ascetic form).
vyuptakeśāya: To the one with shaven hair (Shiva in a different form, possibly symbolic of his many forms).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the thousand-eyed one and to the one who wields hundreds of bows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
sahasrākṣāya: To the thousand-eyed one (representing omnipresence or divine vision).
śatadhanvane: To the one who holds hundreds of bows (symbolizing many weapons or powers).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the lord of the mountains and to the one who pervades all beings.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
giriśāya: To the lord of the mountains (another title of Shiva).
śipiviṣṭāya: To the one who pervades all beings (signifying Shiva's presence in all things).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the most bountiful one and to the wielder of arrows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
mīḍhuṣṭamāya: To the most bountiful one (Shiva as the bestower of blessings).
iṣumate: To the one who holds arrows (Shiva in his fierce warrior form).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the short one and to the small one.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
hrasvāya: To the dwarf or short one (another form of Shiva).
vāmanāya: To the small one (possibly referring to the humble or diminutive form of Shiva).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the large one and to the one who is even greater.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
bṛhate: To the tall or large one (Shiva as the expansive one).
varṣīyase: To the greater one (Shiva as the supreme or larger-than-life deity).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the ancient one and to the ever-growing one.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
vṛddhāya: To the ancient one (Shiva as timeless and eternal).
saṃvṛddhvane: To the one who ever grows (representing constant evolution or expansion).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the foremost one and to the first one.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
agriyāya: To the foremost one (Shiva as the leader).
prathamāya: To the first one (Shiva as the primordial being).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the quick one and to the swift, agile one.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
āśave: To the quick or swift one.
ajirāya: To the fast or agile one (Shiva as speed and movement).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who moves quickly and to the one who brings fortune.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namḥ: Salutations.
śīghriyāya: To the one who moves quickly.
śībhyāya: To the one who brings fortune or wealth.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who is the wave and to the one who resides in water.
contentBlocks.meanings:
nam: Salutations.
ūrmiyāya: To the one who is the wave (symbolizing the flow of life or nature).
āvasvanyāya: To the one who dwells in water (Shiva as the essence of all liquids).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who is the river current and to the one who dwells on islands.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
srotasyāya: To the one who is the river current (symbolizing movement and flow in nature).
dvīpyāya: To the one who dwells on islands (Shiva as present in all places, even remote ones).

contentBlocks.original:
6th Anuvaka
नमो᳚ ज्ये॒ष्ठाय॑ च कनि॒ष्ठाय॑ च॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
6th Anuvaka
namō̎ jyē̠ṣṭhāya̍ cha kani̠ṣṭhāya̍ cha̠
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the eldest and to the youngest.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo jyeṣṭhāya ca kaniṣṭhāya ca: Salutations to the eldest (Jyeṣṭha) and to the youngest (Kaniṣṭha).
namo: Salutations.
jyeṣṭhāya: To the eldest one (referring to the most senior or primordial aspect of Shiva).
kaniṣṭhāya: To the youngest one (representing the newest or most recent aspect of Shiva).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one born before and to the one born after.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ pūrvajāya cāparajāya ca: Salutations to the one born before (Pūrvaja) and to the one born after (Aparaja).
namaḥ: Salutations.
pūrvajāya: To the one born before (the ancient or first-born form of Shiva).
aparajāya: To the one born after (the newer form of Shiva).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who is in the middle and to the immature one.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo madhyamāya cāpagalbhāya ca: Salutations to the one who is in the middle (Madhyama) and to the immature one (Apagalbha).
namo: Salutations.
madhyamāya: To the one in the middle (representing balance or the intermediary form of Shiva).
apagalbhāya: To the one who is immature or youthful.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one at the rear and to the one at the foundation.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo jaghanyāya ca budhniyāya ca: Salutations to the one who is at the rear (Jaghanya) and to the one at the foundation (Budhniya).
namo: Salutations.
jaghanyāya: To the one at the rear or the last one.
budhniyāya: To the one at the foundation (Shiva as the root or base of all things).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who moves swiftly and to the one who moves against.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ sobhyāya ca pratisaryāya ca: Salutations to the one who moves swiftly (Sobha) and to the one who moves against (Pratisarya).
namaḥ: Salutations.
sobhyāya: To the one who moves swiftly (referring to the dynamic or quick aspect of Shiva).
pratisaryāya: To the one who moves against or counteracts.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who rules over death and to the one who provides peace.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo yāmyāya ca kṣemyāya ca: Salutations to the one who rules over death (Yāmya) and to the one who provides peace (Kṣemya).
namo: Salutations.
yāmyāya: To the one who rules over death (Yama, the god of death, also considered an aspect of Shiva).
kṣemyāya: To the one who brings peace and security (Shiva as the protector).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in fertile lands and in barren lands.
contentBlocks.meanings:
nama urvaryāya ca khalyāya ca: Salutations to the one who resides in fertile lands (Urvarya) and in barren lands (Khalya).
nama: Salutations.
urvaryāya: To the one who resides in fertile lands or fields.
khalyāya: To the one who resides in barren or untilled lands.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who is praised and to the one who is at the end.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ ślokyāya cāvasānyāya ca: Salutations to the one who is praised (Ślokya) and to the one who is at the end (Avasanya).
namaḥ: Salutations.
ślokyāya: To the one who is praised or glorified (Shiva as the subject of praise).
avasānyāya: To the one who is at the end (Shiva as the conclusion or the final stage of all things).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in forests and in enclosed spaces.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo vanyāya ca kakṣyāya ca: Salutations to the one who resides in forests (Vanya) and in enclosures (Kakṣya).
namo: Salutations.
vanyāya: To the one who resides in forests or wilderness (Shiva as the protector of nature).
kakṣyāya: To the one who resides in enclosed spaces (gardens or fields).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who hears and to the one who echoes.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ śravāya ca pratiśravāya ca: Salutations to the one who hears (Śrava) and to the one who echoes (Pratiśrava).
namaḥ: Salutations.
śravāya: To the one who hears (Shiva as the omniscient one).
pratiśravāya: To the one who echoes (Shiva as the reverberation of all sounds).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the commander of fast armies and to the one who rides swift chariots.
contentBlocks.meanings:
nama āśuṣeṇāya cāśurathāya ca: Salutations to the commander of fast armies (Āśuṣeṇa) and to the one who rides swift chariots (Āśuratha).
namaḥ: Salutations.
āśuṣeṇāya: To the commander of fast-moving armies (Shiva as the leader of swift forces).
āśurathāya: To the one who rides fast chariots (Shiva as the swift warrior).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the valiant one and to the one who pierces.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ śūrāya cāvabhindate ca: Salutations to the valiant one (Śūra) and to the one who pierces (Avabhindate).
namaḥ: Salutations.
śūrāya: To the valiant or brave one (Shiva as the embodiment of valor).
avabhindate: To the one who pierces or splits.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who wears armor and to the one who protects with a shield.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo varmiṇe ca varūthine ca: Salutations to the one who wears armor (Varmin) and to the one who protects with a shield (Varūthin).
namo: Salutations.
varmiṇe: To the one who wears armor.
varūthine: To the one who protects with a shield.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who wears a helmet and to the one who wears armor.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo bilmine ca kavacine ca: Salutations to the one who wears a helmet (Bilmin) and to the one who wears armor (Kavacin).
namo: Salutations.
bilmine: To the one who wears a helmet.
kavacine: To the one who wears armor.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who is renowned and to the leader of renowned armies.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ śrutāya ca śrutasenāya ca: Salutations to the one who is renowned (Śruta) and to the leader of renowned armies (Śrutasena).
namaḥ: Salutations.
śrutāya: To the one who is renowned or famous (Shiva as the celebrated one).
śrutasenāya: To the leader of renowned armies (Shiva as the head of revered forces).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who sounds like a drum and to the one who strikes down.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
dundubhyāya: To the one who sounds like a drum (referring to the thunderous or resounding form of Rudra).
ahananyāya: To the one who strikes or destroys.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the bold and courageous one and to the one who touches or feels all things.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
dhṛṣṇave: To the bold or courageous one.
pramṛśāya: To the one who touches or feels (possibly referring to Rudra's presence in all things).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the divine messenger and to the one who sends messages.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
dūtāya: To the messenger (Rudra as a divine messenger).
prahitāya: To the one who sends messages.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who carries a sword and to the one who holds a quiver of arrows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
niṣaṅgiṇe: To the one who carries a sword.
iṣudhimate: To the one with a quiver of arrows.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one with sharp arrows and to the wielder of weapons.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
tīkṣṇeṣave: To the one with sharp arrows.
āyudhine: To the wielder of weapons.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one with a beautiful weapon and to the one with a fine bow.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
svāyudhāya: To the one with a personal or beautiful weapon.
sudhanvane: To the one with a fine bow.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in streams and to the one who dwells on paths.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
srutyāya: To the one who dwells in streams or rivers.
pathyāya: To the one who dwells on paths or roads.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in thorny bushes and to the one who resides in hollows or low-lying places.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
kāṭyāya: To the one who resides in thorny bushes or forests.
nīpyāya: To the one who resides in low-lying or hollow places.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in lakes and to the one who dwells in ponds.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
sūdyāya: To the one who dwells in lakes.
sarasyāya: To the one who dwells in ponds.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in flowing rivers and to the one who dwells in still waters.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
nādyāya: To the one who dwells in flowing rivers.
vaiśantāya: To the one who dwells in still waters.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in wells and to the one who resides in water tanks.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
kūpyāya: To the one who resides in wells.
avaṭyāya: To the one who resides in water tanks.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in rainy places and to the one who dwells in places without rain.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
varṣyāya: To the one who dwells in rainy places.
avarṣyāya: To the one who dwells in places where it does not rain.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in the clouds and to the one who resides in lightning.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
meghyāya: To the one who resides in clouds.
vidyutyāya: To the one who resides in lightning.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in quick-moving waters and to the one who resides in heat.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
īghriyāya: To the one who resides in quick-moving waters.
ātapyāya: To the one who resides in heat or the sun.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in the winds and to the one who resides in storms.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
vātyāya: To the one who resides in winds or cyclones.
reṣmiyāya: To the one who resides in storms.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in dwellings and to the protector of dwellings.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
vāstavyāya: To the one who dwells in houses or buildings.
vāstupāya: To the one who protects homes or dwellings.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who is Soma (the divine nectar) and to Rudra (the fierce one).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
somāya: To the one who is Soma (Shiva as the divine nectar or moon).
rudrāya: To Rudra, the fierce one (Shiva in his destructive form).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who is copper-red (Tāmra) and to the one who is reddish-brown (Āruṇa).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namah: Salutations.
tāmrāya: To the one who is copper-red (referring to Rudra's fiery or reddish form).
āruṇāya: To the one who is reddish-brown.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the beneficent one (Śaṅga) and to the lord of all beings (Paśupati).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
śaṅgāya: To the beneficent or gentle one.
paśupataye: To the lord of all beings (Shiva as the protector of all living creatures).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the fierce one (Ugra) and to the formidable one (Bhīma).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
ugrāya: To the fierce one (Shiva in his fearsome form).
bhīmāya: To the formidable or terrifying one.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who strikes nearby (Agrevadha) and to the one who strikes from afar (Dūrevadha).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
agrevadhāya: To the one who strikes nearby.
dūrevadhāya: To the one who strikes from afar.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who kills (Hantā) and to the one who is about to kill (Hanīya).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
hantre: To the one who kills or destroys.
hanīyase: To the one who is about to kill or who causes destruction.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the trees and to those with green leaves (representing the life force within them).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
vṛkṣebhyaḥ: To the trees.
harikeśebhyaḥ: To those with green leaves (representing trees or plants, with the life force in them).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the star (Tārā), the guiding cosmic light.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namah: Salutations.
tārāya: To the star (representing Shiva as the guiding light or the cosmic star).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the source of happiness (Śambhu) and to the source of delight (Mayobhava).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
śambhave: To the source of happiness (another name for Shiva).
mayobhave: To the source of delight or bliss.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the auspicious one (Śaṃkara) and to the one who bestows happiness (Mayaskara).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
śaṃkarāya: To the beneficent or auspicious one (Shiva as the giver of blessings).
mayaskarāya: To the one who bestows happiness or delight.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the auspicious one (Śiva) and to the one who is most auspicious (Śivatara).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
śivāya: To the auspicious one (Shiva as the embodiment of goodness).
śivatarāya: To the one who is even more auspicious or the most benevolent.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in the fords (Tīrthya) and to the one who dwells on the riverbanks (Kūlya).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namah: Salutations.
tīrthyāya: To the one who dwells in the fords (holy crossing places in rivers).
kūlyāya: To the one who dwells on riverbanks.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells at the crossings (Pārya) and to the one who dwells at barriers or dams (Āvārya).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namah: Salutations.
pāryāya: To the one who dwells in crossings (referring to river crossings or transitions).
āvāryāya: To the one who dwells in barriers, dams, or stops.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who helps in crossing over (Prataraṇa) and to the one who helps in ascending or liberating (Uttaraṇa).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namah: Salutations.
prataraṇāya: To the one who helps in crossing over (rivers or difficulties).
uttaraṇāya: To the one who helps in rising or moving upward (ascending or liberating).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who crosses or transcends (Ātārya) and to the one who initiates movement (Ālādya).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
ātāryāya: To the one who crosses or transcends.
ālādyāya: To the one who initiates or begins movement.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in green grass (Śaṣpya) and to the one who is present in foam (Phenya).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
śaṣpyāya: To the one who dwells in green grass (Shiva as present in fresh growth).
phenyāya: To the one who is in foam (Shiva as present in water or foam).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in the sand (Sikatya) and to the one who flows in the current (Pravāhya).
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
sikatyāya: To the one who dwells in the sand.
pravāhyāya: To the one who flows (Shiva as present in the flowing current of water).

contentBlocks.original:
9th Anuvaka
नम॑ इरि॒ण्या॑य च प्रप॒थ्या॑य च॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
9th Anuvaka
nama̍ iri̠ṇyā̍ya cha prapa̠thyā̍ya cha̠
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in deserts and to the one who dwells on paths.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
iriṇyāya: To the one who dwells in deserts or barren lands.
prapathyāya: To the one who dwells on paths or roads.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in gravelly places and to the one who dwells in habitations.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
kiśilāya: To the one who dwells in gravelly or stony places.
kṣayaṇāya: To the one who dwells in habitations or dwellings.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one with matted hair and to the one who is full of wisdom.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
kapardine: To the one with matted hair (Shiva in his ascetic form).
pulastaye: To the wise or learned one (Shiva as the wise sage).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who dwells in cow pens and to the one who dwells in homes.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
goṣṭhyāya: To the one who resides in cow pens or shelters.
gṛhyāya: To the one who resides in homes.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in sleeping places and to the one who resides in sacred spaces.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namah: Salutations.
talpyāya: To the one who resides in sleeping places or beds.
gehyāya: To the one who resides in sacred or private spaces.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in thorny bushes and to the one who resides in caves.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
kāṭyāya: To the one who resides in thorny bushes or woods.
gahvareṣṭhāya: To the one who resides in caves or hidden places.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in the heart and to the one who resides in settled places.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
hṛdayyāya: To the one who resides in the heart (Shiva as the inner consciousness).
niveṣpyāya: To the one who resides in settled places or habitations.

contentBlocks.original: नमः॑ पाग्ं स॒व्या॑य च रज॒स्या॑य च॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: nama̍ḥ pāgṃ sa̠vyā̍ya cha raja̠syā̍ya cha̠
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in muddy places and to the one who resides in dust.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
pāgͫsavyāya: To the one who resides in muddy or marshy places.
rajasyāya: To the one who resides in dust or particles.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in dry places and to the one who resides in green places.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
śuṣkyāya: To the one who resides in dry or barren places.
harityāya: To the one who resides in green or fertile areas.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in lowlands and to the one who resides in hollows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
lopyāya: To the one who resides in low-lying lands.
olapyāya: To the one who resides in hollows or cavities.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in the earth and to the one who resides in the waves.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
ūrvyāya: To the one who resides in the earth.
sūrmyāya: To the one who resides in the waves.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who resides in leafy places and to the one who dwells in leafy coverings.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
parṇyāya: To the one who resides in leafy areas or trees.
parṇaśadyāya: To the one who dwells in leafy coverings or foliage.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who attacks and to the one who strikes.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
apaguramāṇāya: To the one who attacks or assaults.
abhighnate: To the one who strikes or hits.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who afflicts and to the one who torments.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
ākhkhidate: To the one who afflicts or wounds.
prakhkhidate: To the one who torments or causes pain.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you who are the Kirikas, the very hearts of the gods.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo vaḥ: Salutations to you.
kirikebhyaḥ: To the tricksters or deceivers (representing divine trickery or concealment).
devānā hṛdayebhyaḥ: To the hearts of the gods (referring to the core or essence of the gods).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who are emaciated and salutations to those who are searchers.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo: Salutations.
vikṣīṇakebhyaḥ: To those who are emaciated, weakened, or thin.
vicinvatkebhyaḥ: To those who search or explore (those who seek out hidden things).

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to those who are invincible and salutations to those who cause disease.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namaḥ: Salutations.
ānirhatebhyaḥ: To those who are invincible or unconquerable.
āmīvatkebhyaḥ: To those who afflict with disease or sickness.

contentBlocks.original:
10th Anuvaka
द्रापे॒ अन्ध॑सस्पते॒ दरि॑द्र॒न्नील॑लोहित ।
ए॒षा-म्पुरु॑षाणामे॒षा-म्प॑शू॒ना-म्मा भेर्मा-ऽरो॒ मो ए॑षा॒-ङ्किञ्च॒नाम॑मत् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
10th Anuvaka
drāpē̠ andha̍saspatē̠ dari̍dra̠nnīla̍lōhita ।
ē̠ṣā-mpuru̍ṣāṇāmē̠ṣā-mpa̍śū̠nā-mmā bhērmā-'rō̠ mō ē̍ṣā̠-ṅkiñcha̠nāma̍mat ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Rudra, lord of herbs, blue-throated and red-hued one, do not harm these men or these animals. Do not take away their lives or wealth, O lord.
contentBlocks.meanings:
drāpe andhasaspate daridrannīlalohita: O remover of afflictions, lord of food, blue-throated one, and red-hued one.
drāpe: O remover of afflictions.
andhasaspate: O lord of food (provider of nourishment).
daridran: O impoverished one (symbolizing humility).
nīlalohita: O blue-throated and red-hued one (referring to Shiva as Nīlakaṇṭha, the blue-throated one who drank poison, and the red-hued one in his fierce form).
eṣāṃ puruṣāṇāmeṣāṃ paśūnāṃ mā bhermāro mo eṣāṃ kiṃcanāmamat: Do not strike these men, do not harm these animals, and do not destroy anything of theirs.
eṣāṃ puruṣāṇām: These men.
eṣāṃ paśūnām: These animals.
mā bher: Do not strike.
māro: Do not harm.
eṣāṃ kiṃcanāmamat: Do not destroy anything of theirs.

contentBlocks.original:
या ते॑ रुद्र शि॒वा त॒नू-श्शि॒वा वि॒श्वाह॑भेषजी ।
शि॒वा रु॒द्रस्य॑ भेष॒जी तया॑ नो मृड जी॒वसे᳚ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yā tē̍ rudra śi̠vā ta̠nū-śśi̠vā vi̠śvāha̍bhēṣajī ।
śi̠vā ru̠drasya̍ bhēṣa̠jī tayā̍ nō mṛḍa jī̠vasē̎ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: That auspicious and benevolent form of yours, O Rudra, is the healer of the entire world. With that most benevolent form of yours, grant us happiness and long life.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yā te rudra śivā tanūḥ śivā viśvāha bheṣajī: That auspicious form of yours, O Rudra, is all-healing and auspicious.
yā te: That of yours.
rudra: O Rudra.
śivā tanūḥ: Auspicious form.
śivā: Auspicious.
viśvāha: All-healing.
bheṣajī: Healing or remedy.
śivā rudrasya bheṣajī tayā no mṛḍa jīvase: That auspicious healing power of Rudra, with it, make us happy to live.
śivā: Auspicious.
rudrasya: Of Rudra.
bheṣajī: Healing power.
tayā: With that.
no mṛḍa: Make us happy.
jīvase: To live.

contentBlocks.original:
इ॒माग्ं रु॒द्राय॑ त॒वसे॑ कप॒र्दिने᳚ क्ष॒यद्वी॑राय॒ प्रभ॑रामहे म॒तिम् ।
यथा॑ न॒श्शमस॑द्द्वि॒पदे॒ चतु॑ष्पदे॒ विश्व॑-म्पु॒ष्ट-ङ्ग्रामे॑ अ॒स्मिन्नना॑तुरम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
i̠māgṃ ru̠drāya̍ ta̠vasē̍ kapa̠rdinē̎ kṣa̠yadvī̍rāya̠ prabha̍rāmahē ma̠tim ।
yathā̍ na̠śśamasa̍ddvi̠padē̠ chatu̍ṣpadē̠ viśva̍-mpu̠ṣṭa-ṅgrāmē̍ a̠sminnanā̍turam ।
contentBlocks.translation: We offer our minds and hearts to the mighty Rudra, the one with matted hair, the destroyer of enemies, so that, in this village, all beings, both two-legged and four-legged, may flourish and live free from disease.
contentBlocks.meanings:
imārudrāya tavase kapardine kṣayadvīrāya prabharāmahe matim: We offer our thoughts to this mighty Rudra, with matted hair, the destroyer of enemies.
imaḥ: To this.
rudrāya: To Rudra.
tavase: To the mighty one.
kapardine: With matted hair (Shiva in his ascetic form).
kṣayadvīrāya: The destroyer of enemies or evildoers.
prabharāmahe matim: We offer our thoughts.
yathā naḥ śamasaddvipade catuṣpade viśvaṃ puṣṭaṃ grāme āsminnanāturam: May there be peace among two-legged and four-legged beings, and may everything prosper in this village without illness.
yathā naḥ: So that for us.
śamasat: May there be peace.
dvipada: Two-legged beings (humans).
catuṣpade: Four-legged beings (animals).
viśvaṃ puṣṭam: May everything prosper.
grāme āsmin: In this village.
anāturam: Without illness or suffering.

contentBlocks.original:
मृ॒डा नो॑ रुद्रो॒त नो॒ मय॑स्कृधि क्ष॒यद्वी॑राय॒ नम॑सा विधेम ते ।
यच्छ-ञ्च॒ योश्च॒ मनु॑राय॒जे पि॒ता तद॑श्याम॒ तव॑ रुद्र॒ प्रणी॑तौ ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛ̠ḍā nō̍ rudrō̠ta nō̠ maya̍skṛdhi kṣa̠yadvī̍rāya̠ nama̍sā vidhēma tē ।
yachCha-ñcha̠ yōścha̠ manu̍rāya̠jē pi̠tā tada̍śyāma̠ tava̍ rudra̠ praṇī̍tau ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Rudra, make us happy with your blessings. You are the destroyer of enemies. We offer you our salutations. May we obtain the riches and happiness sought by our ancestors under your guidance.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mṛḍā no rudrotano mayaskṛdhi kṣayadvīrāya namasā vidhema te: O Rudra, make us happy, bring us joy, and we offer you salutations to please you, the destroyer of enemies.
mṛḍā: Make us happy.
no rudra: O Rudra.
utano: Also.
mayaskṛdhi: Bring us joy.
kṣayadvīrāya: To the destroyer of enemies.
namasā vidhema te: We offer you salutations to please you.
yacchaṃ ca yośca manurāyaje pitā tadaśyāma tava rudra praṇītau: May we attain that protection which our forefathers and Manu received, led by you, O Rudra.
yacchaṃ: Protection.
yośca: And guidance.
manurāyaje: Received by Manu (the first man) and the ancestors.
pitā: The forefathers.
tadaśyāma: May we attain that.
tava rudra praṇītau: Led by you, O Rudra.

contentBlocks.original:
मा नो॑ म॒हान्त॑मु॒त मा नो॑ अर्भ॒क-म्मा न॒ उक्ष॑न्तमु॒त मा न॑ उक्षि॒तम् ।
मा नो॑-ऽवधीः पि॒तर॒-म्मोत मा॒तर॑-म्प्रि॒या मा न॑स्त॒नुवो॑ रुद्र रीरिषः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mā nō̍ ma̠hānta̍mu̠ta mā nō̍ arbha̠ka-mmā na̠ ukṣa̍ntamu̠ta mā na̍ ukṣi̠tam ।
mā nō̍-'vadhīḥ pi̠tara̠-mmōta mā̠tara̍-mpri̠yā mā na̍sta̠nuvō̍ rudra rīriṣaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Do not harm the elderly, the young, or the infants among us. Do not kill our fathers, mothers, or dear ones. O Rudra, protect us and spare our bodies from harm.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mā no mahāntamuta mā no arbhakaṃ mā na ukṣantamuta mā na ukṣitam: Do not harm our elders, do not harm our children, do not harm our cattle, do not harm our cows.
mā no: Do not harm.
mahāntam: Our elders.
arbhakam: Our children.
ukṣantam: Our cattle (bulls or oxen).
ukṣitam: Our cows.
mā no vadhīḥ pitaraṃ mota mātaraṃ priyā mā nastanuvo rudra rīriṣaḥ: Do not kill our father or mother, do not harm our beloved bodies, O Rudra.
mā no vadhīḥ: Do not kill.
pitaraṃ mota mātaraṃ: Our father or mother.
priyā mā nastanuvo: Do not harm our beloved bodies.
rudra rīriṣaḥ: O Rudra, do not hurt us.

contentBlocks.original:
मा न॑स्तो॒के तन॑ये॒ मा न॒ आयु॑षि॒ मा नो॒ गोषु॒ मा नो॒ अश्वे॑षु रीरिषः ।
वी॒रान्मा नो॑ रुद्र भामि॒तो-ऽव॑धीर्​ह॒विष्मं॑तो॒ नम॑सा विधेम ते ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māna̍stō̠kē tana̍yē̠ mā na̠ āyu̍ṣi̠ mā nō̠ gōṣu̠ mā nō̠ aśvē̍ṣu rīriṣaḥ ।
vī̠rānmā nō̍ rudra bhāmi̠tō-'va̍dhīr​ha̠viṣmaṃ̍tō̠ nama̍sā vidhēma tē ।
contentBlocks.translation: Do not harm our children, our lives, our cattle, or our horses. O Rudra, protector of warriors, do not destroy our heroes. We offer you our worship with respect and devotion.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mānastoke tanaye mā na āyuṣi mā no goṣu mā no aśveṣu rīriṣaḥ: Do not harm our children, do not harm our lifespan, do not harm our cows, do not harm our horses.
mā na stoke: Do not harm our children.
tanaye: Our offspring.
āyuṣi: Our lifespan.
goṣu: Our cows.
aśveṣu: Our horses.
vīrānmā no rudra bhāmito'vadhīrhaviṣmanto namasā vidhema te: Do not kill our brave men, O Rudra. We offer you sacrifices and salutations.
vīrān: Our brave men.
mā no rudra: O Rudra, do not harm us.
bhāmito: O terrifying one.
avadhīḥ: Do not kill.
haviṣmanto: We who offer sacrifices.
namasā vidhema te: We offer you salutations.

contentBlocks.original:
आ॒रात्ते॑ गो॒घ्न उ॒त पू॑रुष॒घ्ने क्ष॒यद्वी॑राय सु॒म्नम॒स्मे ते॑ अस्तु ।
रक्षा॑ च नो॒ अधि॑ च देव ब्रू॒ह्यथा॑ च न॒-श्शर्म॑ यच्छ द्वि॒बर्​हाः᳚ ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ā̠rāttē̍ gō̠ghna u̠ta pū̍ruṣa̠ghnē kṣa̠yadvī̍rāya su̠mnama̠smē tē̍ astu ।
rakṣā̍ cha nō̠ adhi̍ cha dēva brū̠hyathā̍ cha na̠-śśarma̍ yachcha dvi̠bar​hā̎ḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Rudra, who kills cattle and men from afar, we offer our salutations to you, the destroyer of enemies. Protect us and give us your protection and blessings, O mighty one, and grant us happiness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ārātte goghna utta pūruṣaghne kṣayadvīrāya sumnamasme te astu: O Rudra, far from us be the killer of cows and men. May we receive your blessings, O destroyer of enemies.
ārātte: Far from us.
goghna: Killer of cows.
pūruṣaghne: Killer of men.
kṣayadvīrāya: O destroyer of enemies.
sumna: Blessings.
asme te astu: May it be ours.
rakṣā ca no adhi ca deva brūhyathā ca naḥ śarma yaccha dvibarhāḥ: Protect us and bless us, O god, and grant us peace, O wielder of two bows.
rakṣā ca no: Protect us.
adhi ca: And bless us.
deva: O god.
brūhi: Speak (command us).
śarma: Peace or protection.
yaccha: Grant.
dvibarhāḥ: O wielder of two bows.

contentBlocks.original:
स्तु॒हि श्रु॒त-ङ्ग॑र्त॒सदं॒-युँवा॑न-म्मृ॒गन्न भी॒ममु॑पह॒न्तुमु॒ग्रम् ।
मृ॒डा ज॑रि॒त्रे रु॑द्र॒ स्तवा॑नो अ॒न्यन्ते॑ अ॒स्मन्निव॑पन्तु॒ सेनाः᳚ ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stu̠hi śru̠ta-ṅga̍rta̠sada̠ṃ yuvā̍na-mmṛ̠ganna bhī̠mamu̍paha̠ntumu̠gram ।
mṛ̠ḍā ja̍ri̠trē ru̍dra̠ stavā̍nō a̠nyantē̍ a̠smanniva̍pantu̠ sēnā̎ḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Praise the renowned Rudra, who resides in the cave, youthful, fierce like a wild animal, terrifying and powerful. O Rudra, we sing your praises, protect us and drive away the armies of our enemies.
contentBlocks.meanings:
stuhi śrutaṃ gartasadaṃ yuvānaṃ mṛganna bhīmamupahatnumugram: Praise the renowned one, who sits in the cave, who is youthful and terrifying like a wild beast, who strikes down fiercely.
stuhi: Praise.
śrutam: The renowned one.
gartasadam: Who sits in the cave.
yuvānam: Youthful.
mṛganna: Like a wild beast.
bhīmam: Terrifying.
upahatnum: Who strikes down.
ugram: Fierce.
mruḍā jaritre rudra stavāno anyante asmannivapantu senāḥ: Be gracious, O Rudra, to your worshipper. May your armies strike down our enemies.
mruḍā: Be gracious.
jaritre: To your worshipper.
rudra: O Rudra.
stavāno: Praised by me.
anyante: To others.
asmannivapantu: May they strike down.
senāḥ: Your armies.

contentBlocks.original:
परि॑णो रु॒द्रस्य॑ हे॒तिर्वृ॑णक्तु॒ परि॑ त्वे॒षस्य॑ दुर्म॒ति र॑घा॒योः ।
अव॑ स्थि॒रा म॒घव॑द्भ्य-स्तनुष्व॒ मीढ्व॑स्तो॒काय॒ तन॑याय मृडय ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pari̍ṇō ru̠drasya̍ hē̠tirvṛ̍ṇaktu̠ pari̍ tvē̠ṣasya̍ durma̠ti ra̍ghā̠yōḥ ।
ava̍ sthi̠rā ma̠ghava̍dbhya-stanuṣva̠ mīḍhva̍stō̠kāya̠ tana̍yāya mṛḍaya ।
contentBlocks.translation: May Rudra’s weapons avoid us, and may his fierce anger turn away from us. Protect us, O Rudra, and bless our children and our descendants with happiness and well-being.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pariṇo rudrasya hetirvṛṇaktu pari tveṣasya durmatiraghāyoḥ: May the weapons of Rudra be turned away from us, may the harsh thoughts of the fierce one be turned away from us.
pariṇo: Turn away from us.
rudrasya: Rudra's.
hetir: Weapons.
vṛṇaktu: May they protect us.
tveṣasya: The fierce one's.
durmatiḥ: Harsh thoughts.
aghāyoḥ: Of evil.
ava sthirā maghavadbhyastanuṣva mīḍhvastokāya tanayāya mruḍaya: Make firm your blessings for the bountiful, grant us your favor for our sons and children.
ava sthirā: Make firm.
maghavadbhyas: For the bountiful.
tanuṣva: Extend your blessings.
mīḍhvas: O gracious one.
stokāya: For our sons.
tanayāya: For our children.
mruḍaya: Grant us favor.

contentBlocks.original:
मीढु॑ष्टम॒ शिव॑तम शि॒वो नः॑ सु॒मना॑ भव ।
प॒र॒मे वृ॒क्ष आयु॑धन्नि॒धाय॒ कृत्तिं॒-वँसा॑न॒ आच॑र॒ पिना॑क॒-म्बिभ्र॒दाग॑हि ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mīḍhu̍ṣṭama̠ śiva̍tama śi̠vō na̍-ssu̠manā̍ bhava ।
pa̠ra̠mē vṛ̠kṣa āyu̍dhanni̠dhāya̠ kṛtti̠ṃ vasā̍na̠ ācha̍ra̠ pinā̍ka̠-mbibhra̠dāga̍hi ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Rudra, most generous and auspicious one, be kind to us. In the highest tree, place your weapon aside, and come to us, wearing your tiger skin, carrying your Pināka bow, and bestow your grace upon us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mīḍhuṣṭama śivatama śivo naḥ sumanā bhava: O most gracious, most auspicious one, be pleased with us.
mīḍhuṣṭama: O most gracious one.
śivatama: O most auspicious one.
śivo naḥ: Be auspicious to us.
sumanā bhava: Be pleased with us.
parame vrukṣa āyudhaṃ nidhāya kṛttiṃ vasāna ācara pinākaṃ vibhradāgahi: Place your weapon on the highest tree, wearing the skin, come with your bow, O Pināka-bearing one.
parame vrukṣa: On the highest tree.
āyudham nidhāya: Place your weapon.
kṛttiṃ vasāna: Wearing the skin.
ācara: Approach.
pinākaṃ vibhradāgahi: Bearing the bow (Pināka), come.

contentBlocks.original:
विकि॑रिद॒ विलो॑हित॒ नम॑स्ते अस्तु भगवः ।
यास्ते॑ स॒हस्रग्ं॑ हे॒तयो॒न्यम॒स्मन्निव॑पन्तु॒ ताः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viki̍rida̠ vilō̍hita̠ nama̍stē astu bhagavaḥ ।
yāstē̍ sa̠hasragṃ̍ hē̠tayō̠nyama̠smanniva̍pantu̠ tāḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Rudra, the scatterer of enemies, red-hued one, salutations to you, O blessed lord. May your thousand weapons strike down our enemies, but spare us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vikirida vilohita namaste astu bhagavaḥ: O scatterer, red-hued one, salutations to you, O blessed one.
vikirida: O scatterer.
vilohita: O red-hued one.
namaste astu: Salutations to you.
bhagavaḥ: O blessed one.
yāste sahasrahetayo'nyamasmannivapantu tāḥ: Let the thousands of your weapons be directed at others, not at us.
yāste: Your.
sahasrahetayaḥ: Thousands of weapons.
anyam: At others.
asmannivapantu: Let them not be directed at us.
tāḥ: Those (weapons).

contentBlocks.original:
स॒हस्रा॑णि सहस्र॒धा बा॑हु॒वोस्तव॑ हे॒तयः॑ ।
तासा॒मीशा॑नो भगवः परा॒चीना॒ मुखा॑ कृधि ॥ १०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa̠hasrā̍ṇi sahasra̠dhā bā̍hu̠vōstava̍ hē̠taya̍ḥ ।
tāsā̠mīśā̍nō bhagavaḥ parā̠chīnā̠ mukhā̍ kṛdhi ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Your arms hold thousands upon thousands of weapons (sahasrāṇi sahasradhā bāhuvostava hētayaḥ) — O Bhagavān, master of them all (tāsām īśānaḥ), turn them away from us — let their terrible force face away and be directed elsewhere (parāchīnā mukhā kṛdhi)!
contentBlocks.meanings:
sahasrāṇi sahasradhā bāhuvostava hetayaḥ: Your arms hold thousands upon thousands of weapons.
sahasrāṇi: Thousands.
sahasradhā: Thousands upon thousands.
bāhuvoḥ tava: Your arms.
hetayaḥ: Weapons.
tāsāmīśāno bhagavaḥ parācīnā mukhā kṛdhi: O blessed one, lord of all, turn their faces away from us.
tāsām: Of those (weapons).
īśāno: O lord.
bhagavaḥ: O blessed one.
parācīnā mukhā: Turn their faces.
kṛdhi: Make them face away.

contentBlocks.original:
11th Anuvaka
स॒हस्रा॑णि सहस्र॒शो ये रु॒द्रा अधि॒ भूम्या᳚म् ।
तेषाग्ं॑ सहस्रयोज॒ने-ऽव॒धन्वा॑नि तन्मसि ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
11th Anuvaka
sa̠hasrā̍ṇi sahasra̠śō yē ru̠drā adhi̠ bhūmyā̎m ।
tēṣāg̍ṃ sahasrayōja̠nē-'va̠dhanvā̍ni tanmasi ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Rudra, you have thousands of weapons in your arms. O lord, make them face away from us and protect us.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sahasrāṇi sahasraśo ye rudrā adhi bhūmyām: Thousands upon thousands of Rudras are on the earth.
sahasrāṇi sahasraśaḥ: Thousands upon thousands.
ye rudrāḥ: The Rudras.
adhi bhūmyām: On the earth.
teṣāsahasrayojane'vadhanvāni tanmasi: We remove their bows at a thousand yojanas' distance.
teṣāṃ: Of theirs.
sahasra yojane: At a distance of a thousand yojanas (a unit of distance).
avadhanvāni: Their bows.
tanmasi: We remove.

contentBlocks.original:
अ॒स्मिन्म॑ह॒त्य॑र्ण॒वें᳚-ऽतरि॑क्षे भ॒वा अधि॑ ।
नील॑ग्रीवा-श्शिति॒कण्ठा᳚-श्श॒र्वा अ॒धः, क्ष॑माच॒राः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
a̠sminma̍ha̠tya̍rṇa̠vē̎m-'tari̍kṣē bha̠vā adhi̍ ।
nīla̍grīvā-śśiti̠kaṇṭhā̎-śśa̠rvā a̠dhaḥ, kṣa̍mācha̠rāḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: In this vast ocean of space, Rudra dwells. His blue throat and white neck shine. He moves across the earth, fierce and mighty.
contentBlocks.meanings:
asmin mahatyarṇave'ntarikṣe bhavā adhi: In this great ocean, in the atmosphere, you dwell.
asmin: In this.
mahatya arṇave: Great ocean.
antarikṣe: In the atmosphere (the sky).
bhavā adhi: You dwell.
nīlagrīvāḥ śitikaṇṭhāḥ śarvā adhaḥ kṣamācarāḥ: The blue-throated, white-necked, and fierce ones roam the earth below.
nīlagrīvāḥ: The blue-throated ones (Shiva as Nīlakaṇṭha).
śitikaṇṭhāḥ: The white-necked ones.
śarvāḥ: The fierce ones (another form of Rudra).
adhaḥ kṣamācarāḥ: Roam the earth below.

contentBlocks.original:
नील॑ग्रीवा-श्शिति॒कण्ठा॒ दिवग्ं॑ रु॒द्रा उप॑श्रिताः ।
ये वृ॒क्षेषु॑ स॒स्पिञ्ज॑रा॒ नील॑ग्रीवा॒ विलो॑हिताः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nīla̍grīvā-śśiti̠kaṇṭhā̠ divag̍ṃ ru̠drā upa̍śritāḥ ।
yē vṛ̠kṣēṣu̍ sa̠spiñja̍rā̠ nīla̍grīvā̠ vilō̍hitāḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: The blue-throated, white-necked Rudras dwell in the skies. The Rudras in the trees, yellowish and reddish-hued, also roam, fierce in their appearance.
contentBlocks.meanings:
nīlagrīvāḥ śitikaṇṭhā divarudrā upaśritāḥ: The blue-throated, white-necked Rudras dwell in the sky.
nīlagrīvāḥ: The blue-throated ones.
śitikaṇṭhāḥ: The white-necked ones.
diva rudrāḥ: The Rudras of the sky.
upaśritāḥ: They dwell.
ye vṛkṣeṣu saspiṃjarā nīlagrīvā vilohitāḥ: Those blue-throated, red-hued Rudras dwell in trees and in the tender grass.
ye vṛkṣeṣu: Those who are in the trees.
saspiṃjarāḥ: Dwelling in the tender grass.
nīlagrīvāḥ: The blue-throated ones.
vilohitāḥ: The red-hued ones.

contentBlocks.translation: Those who are the lords of all beings, armed with arrows, and bearing matted hair.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ye bhūtānām adhipatayaḥ: Those who are the lords of all beings.
viśikhāsaḥ: Armed with arrows.
kapardinaḥ: With matted hair (ascetic form of Shiva).

contentBlocks.original: ये अन्ने॑षु वि॒विध्य॑न्ति॒ पात्रे॑षु॒ पिब॑तो॒ जनान्॑ ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: yē annē̍ṣu vi̠vidhya̍nti̠ pātrē̍ṣu̠ piba̍tō̠ janān̍ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Those who pierce people while they eat and drink from vessels.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ye anneṣu: Those who pierce people while they eat.
pātreṣu: In vessels (referring to eating and drinking).
pibataḥ janān: As people drink.

contentBlocks.translation: Those who protect the paths, who are born of the earth, and who fight in battles.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ye pathāṃ: Those who guard the paths.
pathirakṣaya: Protectors of the roads.
ailabṛdāḥ: Born of the earth.
yavyudhaḥ: Who fight in battles.

contentBlocks.translation: Those who move through holy places, armed with spears and swords.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ye tīrthāni pracaranti: Those who move in holy places (pilgrimage sites).
sṛkāvantaḥ: Armed with spears.
