niṣaṅgiṇaḥ: Armed with swords.

contentBlocks.original: य ए॒ताव॑न्तश्च॒ भूयाग्ं॑सश्च॒ दिशो॑ रु॒द्रा वि॑तस्थि॒रे ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: ya ē̠tāva̍ntaścha̠ bhūyāg̍ṃsaścha̠ diśō̍ ru̠drā vi̍tasthi̠rē ।
contentBlocks.translation: These Rudras, both vast and numerous, have spread out across all directions.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ya etāvantaḥ: These vast (Rudras).
ca bhūyāsaḥ: And numerous.
diśaḥ: In all directions.
rudrā vitasthire: The Rudras spread out.

contentBlocks.translation: At a distance of a thousand yojanas from them, we unstring their bows.
contentBlocks.meanings:
teṣām: Of theirs.
sahasrayojane: At a distance of a thousand yojanas.
avadhanvāni: Their bows.
tanmasi: We remove.

contentBlocks.original: नमो॑ रु॒ध्रेभ्यो॒ ये पृ॑थि॒व्यां-येँ᳚-ऽन्तरि॑क्षे॒ ये दि॒वि येषा॒मन्नं॒-वाँतो॑ व॒र्​ष॒मिष॑व॒स्तेभ्यो॒ दश॒ प्राची॒र्दश॑ दक्षि॒णा दश॑ प्र॒तीची॒-र्दशो-दी॑ची॒-र्दशो॒र्ध्वास्तेभ्यो॒ नम॒स्ते नो॑ मृडयन्तु॒ ते य-न्द्वि॒ष्मो यश्च॑ नो॒ द्वेष्टि॒ तं-वोँ॒ जम्भे॑ दधामि॥ ११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: namō̍ ru̠dhrēbhyō̠ yē pṛ̍thi̠vyāṃ yē̎-'ntari̍kṣē̠ yē di̠vi yēṣā̠manna̠ṃ vātō̍ va̠r​ṣa̠miṣa̍va̠stēbhyō̠ daśa̠ prāchī̠rdaśa̍ dakṣi̠ṇā daśa̍ pra̠tīchī̠-rdaśō-dī̍chī̠-rdaśō̠rdhvāstēbhyō̠ nama̠stē nō̍ mṛḍayantu̠ tē ya-ndvi̠ṣmō yaścha̍ nō̠ dvēṣṭi̠ taṃ vō̠ jambhē̍ dadhāmi ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the Rudras on earth, in the atmosphere, and in the sky, whose food is wind, rain, and arrows — ten to each direction and ten above — may you make us happy, and whoever we hate or whoever hates us, I place them into your jaws.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo rudrebhyo: Salutations to the Rudras.
ye pṛthivyām: Who are on earth.
ye antarikṣe: Who are in the atmosphere.
ye divi: Who are in the sky.
yeṣām annaṃ: Whose food is.
vātaḥ: Air (wind).
varṣaḥ: Rain.
iṣavaḥ: Arrows.
daśa prācīḥ: Ten to the east.
daśa dakṣiṇāḥ: Ten to the south.
daśa pratīcīḥ: Ten to the west.
daśa udīcīḥ: Ten to the north.
daśa ūrdhvāḥ: Ten above.
namaste: Salutations to you.
no mṛḍayantu: May you make us happy.
te yaṃ dviṣmaḥ: Whoever we hate.
yaśca no dveṣṭi: And whoever hates us.
taṃ vo jambhe dadhāmi: I place them into your jaws.

contentBlocks.transliteration:
trya̍mbakaṃ yajāmahē suga̠ndhi-mpu̍ṣṭi̠vardha̍nam ।
u̠rvā̠ru̠kami̍va̠ bandha̍nānmṛtyō̍rmukṣīya̠ mā-'mṛtā̎t ।
contentBlocks.translation: We worship the three-eyed lord (Tryambaka), fragrant and nourishing, who increases our vitality. Like a cucumber detaches from its vine, may we be freed from the bonds of death, but not from immortality.
contentBlocks.meanings:
tryambakaṃ yajāmahe sugandhiṃ puṣṭivardhanam: We worship the three-eyed one, fragrant, and increasing nourishment.
tryambakam: The three-eyed one (Shiva).
yajāmahe: We worship.
sugandhim: The fragrant one.
puṣṭivardhanam: The increaser of nourishment.
urvārukamiva bandhanānmṛtyormukṣīya mā'mṛtāt: As a cucumber is severed from its bondage, may I be freed from death, but not from immortality.
urvārukam iva: As a cucumber.
bandhanāt: From its bondage.
mṛtyoḥ mukṣīya: May I be freed from death.
mā'mṛtāt: But not from immortality.

contentBlocks.original: यो रु॒द्रो अ॒ग्नौ यो अ॒प्सु य ओष॑धीषु॒ यो रु॒द्रो विश्वा॒ भुव॑ना वि॒वेश॒ तस्मै॑ रु॒द्राय॒ नमो॑ अस्तु ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: yō ru̠drō a̠gnau yō a̠psu ya ōṣa̍dhīṣu̠ yō ru̠drō viśvā̠ bhuva̍nā vi̠vēśa̠ tasmai̍ ru̠drāya̠ namō̍ astu ।
contentBlocks.translation: That Rudra who dwells in fire, in the waters, and in the plants, and who has entered into all the worlds — salutations to that Rudra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yo rudro agnau yo apsu ya oṣadhīṣu: That Rudra who is in the fire, in the waters, and in the plants.
yo rudraḥ: That Rudra.
agnau: In the fire.
apsu: In the waters.
oṣadhīṣu: In the plants.
yo rudro viśvā bhuvanā''viveśa tasmai rudrāya namo astu: That Rudra who has entered into all the worlds, salutations to that Rudra.
yo rudraḥ: That Rudra.
viśvā bhuvanā''viveśa: Who has entered into all the worlds.
tasmai rudrāya: To that Rudra.
namo astu: Salutations.

contentBlocks.original: तमु॑ ष्टु॒हि॒ य-स्स्वि॒षुस्सु॒धन्वा॒ यो विश्व॑स्य॒ क्षय॑ति भेष॒जस्य॑ ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: tamu̍ ṣṭu̠hi̠ ya-ssvi̠ṣussu̠dhanvā̠ yō viśva̍sya̠ kṣaya̍ti bhēṣa̠jasya̍ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Praise that Rudra who is armed with arrows, who holds a good bow, and who is the lord of all remedies.
contentBlocks.meanings:
tamuṣṭuhi yaḥ sviṣuḥ sudhanvā yo viśvasya kṣayati bheṣajasya: Praise that Rudra, who is armed with arrows, who holds a good bow, and who is the destroyer of all remedies.
tam uṣṭuhi: Praise that one.
yaḥ sviṣuḥ: Who is armed with arrows.
sudhanvā: Who holds a good bow.
viśvasya kṣayati bheṣajasya: The destroyer of all remedies.

contentBlocks.translation: Let us invoke Rudra with salutations for a peaceful mind, honouring the god who is both divine and mighty, who bestows blessings.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yakṣvāmahe saumanasāya rudraṃ namobhirdevamasuraṃ duvasya: Let us invoke Rudra for a peaceful mind with salutations, who is both god and demon, who brings blessings.
yakṣvāmahe: Let us invoke.
saumanasāya: For a peaceful mind.
rudram: Rudra.
namobhiḥ: With salutations.
devam asuram: The god and demon.
duvasya: Who brings blessings.

contentBlocks.translation: This hand of mine is blessed, and this hand of mine is even more blessed.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ayaṃ me hasto bhagavānayaṃ me bhagavattaraḥ: This hand of mine is blessed, this hand of mine is even more blessed.
ayaṃ me hastaḥ: This hand of mine.
bhagavān: Is blessed.
ayaṃ me bhagavattaraḥ: This hand of mine is even more blessed.

contentBlocks.translation: This hand of mine is the universal healer, and it is the auspicious touch.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ayaṃ me viśvabheṣajo'ya śivābhimarśanaḥ: This hand of mine is the healer of all, it is the auspicious touch.
ayaṃ me: This hand of mine.
viśvabheṣajaḥ: Is the healer of all.
śivābhimarśanaḥ: It is the auspicious touch.

contentBlocks.original: ये ते॑ स॒हस्र॑म॒युत॒-म्पाशा॒ मृत्यो॒ मर्त्या॑य॒ हन्त॑वे ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: yē tē̍ sa̠hasra̍ma̠yuta̠-mpāśā̠ mṛtyō̠ martyā̍ya̠ hanta̍vē ।
contentBlocks.translation: O Death, those snares of yours, thousands and tens of thousands in number, are meant for slaying mortals.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ye te sahasramayutaṃ pāśā mṛtyo martyāya hantave: O Death, those snares of yours, thousands and tens of thousands, are for slaying mortals.
ye te pāśāḥ: Those snares of yours.
sahasra mayutaṃ: Thousands and tens of thousands.
mṛtyo: O Death.
martyāya hantave: Are for slaying mortals.

contentBlocks.translation: By the power of the sacrifice, we cast away all those snares entirely.
contentBlocks.meanings:
tān yajñasya māyayā sarvānava yajāmahe: By the power of the sacrifice, we cast away all those snares.
tān: Those.
yajñasya māyayā: By the power of the sacrifice.
sarvān ava yajāmahe: We cast them all away.

contentBlocks.translation: Oblation to Death, oblation to Death.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mṛtyave svāhā mṛtyave svāhā: Oblation to death, oblation to death.
mṛtyave svāhā: Oblation to death.

contentBlocks.original: ओ-न्नमो भगवते रुद्राय विष्णवे मृत्यु॑र्मे पा॒हि ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: ō-nnamō bhagavatē rudrāya viṣṇavē mṛtyu̍rmē pā̠hi ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ, salutations to the blessed Rudra-Viṣṇu; protect me from death.
contentBlocks.meanings:
oṃ namo bhagavate rudrāya viṣṇave mṛtyurme pāhi: Oṃ, salutations to the blessed one, Rudra-Viṣṇu, protect me from death.
oṃ namo bhagavate: Oṃ, salutations to the blessed one.
rudrāya viṣṇave: To Rudra-Viṣṇu (a unified form of the two gods).
mṛtyurme pāhi: Protect me from death.

contentBlocks.translation: O Rudra, you are the knot that binds the life forces together — do not sever them.
contentBlocks.meanings:
prāṇānāṃ granthirasi rudro mā viśāntakaḥ: O Rudra, you are the knot of life forces, do not cut them off.
prāṇānāṃ granthiḥ asi: You are the knot of life forces.
rudraḥ: O Rudra.
mā viśāntakaḥ: Do not cut them off.

contentBlocks.translation: Nourish yourself with this food.
contentBlocks.meanings:
tenānnenāpyāyasva: Nourish yourself with this food.
tena annena: With this food.
apyāyasva: Nourish yourself.

contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Rudra-Viṣṇu; protect me from death.
contentBlocks.meanings:
namo rudrāya viṣṇave mṛtyurme pāhi: Salutations to Rudra-Viṣṇu, protect me from death.
namo rudrāya viṣṇave: Salutations to Rudra-Viṣṇu.
mṛtyurme pāhi: Protect me from death.

contentBlocks.translation:
Om, I invoke the eternal Lord Shiva.
Om, may there be peace, peace, peace.
contentBlocks.meanings: सदा (sadā) - alwaysशिवः (śivaḥ) - ShivaThe word "Oṃ" is a primordial sound and a sacred syllable in Hinduism that signifies the essence of the ultimate reality or consciousness (Brahman). It is a universal symbol that represents the beginning of creation, sustenance, and dissolution."Śāntiḥ" means "peace." Repeating the word "śāntiḥ" three times has a profound significance in Vedic prayers and rituals. The repetition is intended to invoke peace on three levels:Ādhyātmika Śāntiḥ (Personal peace): This refers to peace within oneself. It is a prayer to remove internal disturbances such as mental stress, emotional turmoil, and physical discomfort.Ādhibhautika Śāntiḥ (Environmental peace): This refers to peace from external factors or disturbances caused by other beings or natural forces like accidents, animals, or environmental disasters.Ādhidaivika Śāntiḥ (Divine peace): This refers to peace from cosmic or supernatural forces, which may include natural calamities or disturbances from divine or otherworldly beings.By chanting "śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ," one prays for complete harmony and peace across all dimensions of existence: internally, externally, and universally.
